preamble: The shift from the voiceless aspirated bilabial stop [pʰ] to its palatalized counterpart [pʲ] is a form of secondary palatalization. It typically occurs before high front vowels or palatal glides, where the aspiration interacts with the palatal constriction.

examples.note: Stages in the development of soft consonants before front vowels.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
